name: Greenville Federal
description_short: Greenville Federal is a community bank based in Greenville, OH, offering personal and business checking, savings, credit cards, loans, and HSAs with a focus on local service and personalized banking solutions.
description_long:
Greenville Federal operates as a traditional community bank headquartered in Greenville, Ohio. The bank positions itself as "The Bank That Inspires" and emphasizes tailored banking solutions for both personal and business customers. With an NMLS ID of 586410 and routing number 242272159, it maintains multiple banking center locations across Greenville, Troy, and Tipp City.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, CDs, Health Savings Accounts (HSAs), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and credit cards. For business customers, they provide business checking, business savings accounts, and competitive deposit rates. Their digital offerings include online banking platforms and a mobile app branded as "GF Mobile," which they describe as an "ALL NEW" experience for card management. The bank also provides loan products across multiple categories and advertises "the best interest rates" as a core offering.

Greenville Federal distinguishes itself through community engagement and localized decision-making. The bank highlights relationships with local businesses through case studies (such as Arcon Builders, a 5-year customer), features a quarterly "Small Business Spotlight" program highlighting local entrepreneurs, and demonstrates employee involvement in community leadership roles. They maintain a physical branch network with identifiable banking center managers at specific locations, positioning themselves as an alternative to larger national banks through personalized service and local knowledge.

The bank appears well-established and compliant, with accessibility features (AudioEye enabled website), regulatory compliance (NMLS registration), and professional online infrastructure. However, as a community bank with limited geographic presence, their product competitiveness, interest rates, and service capabilities would need direct comparison with larger regional or national competitors. The website provides limited specific detail on actual interest rates, fees, or loan terms, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly for detailed pricing. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal and business loans with competitive rates
GF Mobile app for card management
ATM access and Allpoint ATM network
Click-Through product demos without account requirement
pros: Maintains multiple physical banking center locations in Greenville, Troy, and Tipp City for in-person service
Offers both personal and business banking products from checking to loans under one institution
Provides digital banking through online platform and dedicated GF Mobile app for card management
Demonstrates active community engagement through business spotlights and employee leadership involvement
Offers specialized savings products including Health Savings Accounts and Individual Retirement Accounts
Bank leadership is locally identifiable with named banking center managers at each location
Website is AudioEye-enabled for accessibility compliance
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Ohio communities may not serve customers outside service area
Website provides no specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules—requires direct contact for pricing details
No mention of competitive advantages like no-fee checking, higher savings rates, or promotional offers
As a smaller community bank, may have limited product innovation or digital features compared to larger competitors
No information about customer support availability (phone hours, chat support, response times) on the website
best_for: Small business owners in Greenville, Troy, or Tipp City seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-makers
Consumers in the tri-city area prioritizing in-person service and community bank values over large national chains
Self-employed individuals and small business owners needing both personal and business accounts at one institution
Local residents seeking HSAs, IRAs, and other specialized savings products with personalized guidance
diagnosis: Greenville Federal is best for small business owners and personal banking customers within Greenville, Troy, and Tipp City, Ohio who value local relationships and in-person service over digital-first banking. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this is a community bank with physical presence only in three Ohio communities, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those requiring 24/7 digital-only banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
